rationale: The hip-style roofs across this Thornton, CO townhome complex display moderately long eave-to-ground shadows for the autumn sun angle at ~39.9°N latitude, consistent with a 6:12 pitch. The ridge lines sit clearly inward from the eave footprint but the visible roof plane area is still substantial, indicating a mid-range pitch rather than a shallow or very steep one. The hip triangular faces at each building corner show moderate vertical prominence, and internal valley/ridge shadow relief confirms meaningful elevation between intersecting roof planes. The Colorado Front Range climate and standard production-townhome construction practices strongly favor 5:12–7:12; the shadow geometry and roof-plane proportions most closely match 6:12 among the available choices.
